behavior_summary: This script implements a world object 'WOUNDTOR' that visually toggles between two frames while an actor is inside its fast-area and responds to player inspection and hits.

When an actor enters the object's fast-area, the object spawns a background process that repeatedly reads the item's frame, flips it between two values (0 and 1), optionally plays a special SFX on one transition, and schedules a randomized delay before the next toggle.
When the actor leaves the fast-area the script invokes a cleanup helper to stop/remove the spawned background process.
When a player 'looks' (inspects) the object the script queries the current frame and issues a short bark describing the Torax as either wounded or healed depending on that frame.
When the object is hit (gotHit), it plays a hit sound effect.
notable_control_flow: U8P_WOUNDTOR::enterFastArea: triggered by area entry; spawns U8P_WOUNDTOR::013A as a background process (spawn).

U8P_WOUNDTOR::013A: background loop — suspend, call Item::getFrame, compute and set the opposite frame via Item::setFrame, play SFX on one direction, compute randomized delay via urandom, spawn FREE::1C19 with that delay (acts as a timer), then suspend and loop until killed.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::leaveFastArea: triggered by area exit; calls FREE::2A56 to perform cleanup/stop of the spawned/timed behavior.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::look: on a U8P_WOUNDTOR::look event, calls Item::getFrame then Item::bark with either a 'Healed Torax ' or 'Wounded Torax ' message based on the frame.
U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit: on hit event, calls a small playSFX routine with fixed parameters.
Loop/frame progression inside U8P_WOUNDTOR::013A; treated as control-flow, not explicit internal call.
open_questions: Which numeric frame value (0 or 1) definitively corresponds to 'wounded' vs 'healed' — the code branches, but the mapping is inferred and not explicitly labeled.
Exact behavior and interface of FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56: they appear to be timer/spawn cleanup helpers, but their full semantics (arguments beyond delay and implicit pid usage) remain inferred.
Precise meanings and units of spawn delay calculation (urandom range and added offset) and how often the toggle runs in real time.
Full signature/semantics of U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit parameters (two numeric args seen) — are they damage and attacker id, and are they used elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor 'U8P_WOUNDTOR::look' (inspect) event by querying the item's display frame and emitting a short descriptive bark indicating a wounded or healed Torax state.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player or relevant actor looks at / inspects the item (implicit 'this' at BP+06h)

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::look
function_map.role: Handle examine/U8P_WOUNDTOR::look events; decides and emits item description based on Item::getFrame.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger that sets up state and spawns the background toggling process.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit trigger that cleans up/stops the spawned background process.

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::gotHit
function_map.role: On-hit handler that plays a hit sound effect; accepts two numeric args (likely damage and attacker id).

function_map.name: U8P_WOUNDTOR::013A
function_map.role: Spawned background process that loops to toggle the item's frame, play SFX on some transitions, and schedule randomized delays via a timer spawn.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the background toggling behavior when actor enters fast-area.
Request cleanup/stop of the spawned background/timer behavior on area exit.

external_calls.purpose: Query the current display/animation frame to decide the bark text.
Emit a short text message ('Healed Torax ' or 'Wounded Torax ') to the player inspecting the item.
Read current frame to determine whether to toggle.
Set the item's frame to the alternate state (toggle 0<->1).
Play a larger/special sound effect when switching frame in a particular direction.
Play a small/hit sound effect when object is struck.
Generate a pseudo-random value to vary the delay between frame toggles.
Spawned as a timer/scheduler with a computed delay to suspend/resume or schedule the next toggle.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a world object 'WOUNDTOR' that visually toggles between two frames while an actor is inside its fast-area and responds to player inspection and hits.
